etymology.description: semantic 癶 (stretch out) + phonetic 殳 (a type of weapon) -> to issue
etymology.interpretation: The character 發(fā, "issue") combines the semantic element 癶(bó, "stretch out") representing action or movement, with the phonetic element 殳(shū, "a type of weapon") which provides the sound. The idea of stretching out or moving forward aligns with the notion of issuing or sending forth, whether it be an object, action, or command.
etymology.simplification: In the simplified form 发, the component 殳 (shū, "a type of weapon") was replaced with another element that retains the phonetic sound to reduce the number of strokes.

examples.chinese: 公司今天发布了新的政策(公司今天發佈了新的政策)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī jīntiān fābù le xīn de zhèngcè.
examples.english: The company issued a new policy today.

examples.chinese: 他发了一封邀请函給我(他發了一封邀請函給我)。
examples.pinyin: Tā fāle yī fēng yāoqǐng hán gěi wǒ.
examples.english: He issued an invitation letter to me.

examples.chinese: 老师决定不对这个错误发言(老師決定不對這個錯誤發言)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī juédìng bù duì zhège cuòwù fāyán.
examples.english: The teacher decided not to issue a comment on this mistake.
